name: Triple Berry Jam
recipeInstructions:
(about 3 pints fully ripe strawberries, 1-1/2 pints fully ripe red raspberries and 1 pint fully ripe blackberries)
Thoroughly crush the strawberries in one layer at a time. Measure 2-1/2 cups into a 6- or 8-quart saucepot.
Thoroughly crush the raspberries in one layer at a time. Measure 1-1/2 cups into the saucepot.
Thoroughly crush the blackberries in one layer at a time. Measure 1 cup into the saucepot.
Measure the sugar into a separate bowl, scraping excess with a spatula to level.
Stir fruit pectin into the fruit mixture in the saucepot and add margarine.
Bring mixture to full rolling boil on high heat, stirring constantly. Quickly stir in all sugar.
Maintain full rolling boil for exactly 1 minute, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
Use a metal spoon to skim off any foam.
Quickly ladle into hot sterilized jars, filling to within 1/8 inch of the top.
Wipe jar rims and threads clean.
Cover with two-piece lids and tighten bands.
Invert jars for 5 minutes, then turn upright.
After jars are cool, check seals.
Or follow water bath processing method recommended by USDA.
Makes 8 cups or 8 (1-cup) jars.
